labormarket 0.1.0
  • Get Started
    • Introduction
    • Analyzing a Simulated Labor Market
  • Reference

Simulate a Labor Market with Gender Bias

Source: R/simlabormarket.R
simlabormarket.Rd

This function creates a simulated labor market with parameters that control the number of firm types, the fraction of females in the labor market, percentage of movers, etc. The simulated panel includes worker and firm fixed effects, match effects, and optionally Mincer-style human capital variables (age, education, experience).

Usage

simlabormarket(
  nk = 6,
  ratiog = 0.45,
  lambda = 0.05,
  nl = 10,
  nt = 4,
  ni = 1e+05,
  mincer = FALSE
)

Arguments

nk

Integer >= 2. Number of firm types or clusters.

ratiog

Numeric in (0, 1). Fraction of females in the labor market.

lambda

Numeric in (0, 1). Probability of a worker moving firms each period.

nl

Integer >= 2. Number of worker types or clusters.

nt

Integer >= 2. Number of time periods.

ni

Positive integer. Number of individuals.

mincer

Logical. If TRUE, wages are generated using a Mincer equation (returns on education, experience, and experience squared) together with worker and firm random effects. Default is FALSE.

Value

A LaborMarket S4 object containing the simulated panel and parameters.

See also

lmbias() for correcting limited mobility bias using the simulated data, LaborMarket for the S4 class definition.

Examples

# Simulate a small labor market
lm_obj <- simlabormarket(nk = 3, nl = 3, nt = 3, ni = 100, lambda = 0.3)
#> -- LaborMarket simulation ---
#> 
#>   Parameters
#>     Firm types (nk):     3 
#>     Worker types (nl):   3 
#>     Time periods (nt):   3 
#>     Individuals (ni):    100 
#>     Female ratio:        0.45 
#>     Mobility (lambda):   0.3 
#> 
#>   Summary statistics
#>     Observations:        300 
#>     Mean wage:           4.48 
#>     Mean firm effect:    1.684 
#>     Mean worker effect:  2.794 
#> 

# With Mincer wage equation
lm_mincer <- simlabormarket(nk = 3, nl = 3, nt = 3, ni = 100,
                             lambda = 0.3, mincer = TRUE)
#> -- LaborMarket simulation ---
#> 
#>   Parameters
#>     Firm types (nk):     3 
#>     Worker types (nl):   3 
#>     Time periods (nt):   3 
#>     Individuals (ni):    100 
#>     Female ratio:        0.45 
#>     Mobility (lambda):   0.3 
#> 
#>   Summary statistics
#>     Observations:        300 
#>     Mean wage:           5.916 
#>     Mean firm effect:    1.733 
#>     Mean worker effect:  3.006 
#>     Mean age:            35.2 
#>     Mean education:      16.1 
#>     Mean experience:     9 
#>
